```javascript
// Finds the index of the last element <= X in nums[0...N-1]
// Returns -1 if all elements are > X.
function find_j(nums, X) {
    let N = nums.length;
    let low = 0;
    let high = N - 1;
    let result_index = -1;

    while (low <= high) {
        let mid = Math.floor((low + high) / 2);
        if (nums[mid] <= X) {
            result_index = mid;
            low = mid + 1; // Try to find a larger index
        } else {
            high = mid - 1;
        }
    }
    return result_index;
}
```
